text:
INDICES — VIX 25.3, pulling back from 52-week high of 52.3, signaling elevated but easing fear. Daily returns ranked: IWM +1.22%, IJH +0.87%, QQQ +0.66%, DIA +0.64%, SPY +0.56%, RSP +0.48%. Small caps outperformed large caps decisively. 10-year proxy TLT down 3.1% over 20d; HYG off 1.4% suggesting mild credit stress.

SECTORS — Ranked by 1d return: XLB +2.0%, XLY +1.0%, XLV +1.0%, XLI +0.7%, XLK +0.5%, XLP +0.5%, XLC +0.3%, XLU +0.3%, XLF +0.1%, XLRE -0.1%, XLE -0.4%. Top 3 industries: ROBO +1.9%, ARGT +1.7%, XHE +0.8%. Bottom 3 industries not separately ranked by 1d in data; weakest 20d sectors are XLP -7.9%, XLV -7.0%, XLB -6.9%.

THE 7s — Ranked by 20d: Energy 7 +13.1%, Software 7 +2.1%, Cloud 7 +0.6%, Semi 7 -0.5%, Buildout 7 -0.7%, New Staples 7 -1.3%, Defense 7 -2.8%, Toll Road 7 -3.0%, Insurance 7 -3.6%, Power 7 -3.9%, Health 7 -4.6%, Bank 7 -5.4%, Fab 7 -6.9%, Freight 7 -7.0%, Old Staples 7 -8.2%, Industrial 7 -8.5%, Medtech 7 -8.5%, Housing 7 -12.0%, New Metals 7 -12.1%, Old Metals 7 -19.2%. Energy 7 led by DVN +18.9%; Old Metals 7 crushed by GOLD -23.2%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 52.1, neutral. Volume spikes: IJK 16.9x, IJS 12.8x, XHE 4.4x, ARGT 3.0x, ROBO 2.5x. Strongest 20d momentum: XLE +11.1%, Optical Comms +33.2%. Weakest: XLP -7.9%, Old Metals 7 -19.2%. RSP -4.87% vs SPY -4.98% over 20d — breadth expanding marginally.
